label: a!flatten
documentation:
Converts an array that contains other arrays into an array of single items.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!flatten([array])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!flatten(merge({1,2},{11, 12}))
```

**Result:**
```
{1, 11, 2, 12}
```

label: a!update
documentation:
Inserts new values or replaces existing values at the specified index or field name and returns the resulting updated data.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!update([data], [index], [value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!update(a!map(a: 1, b: 2), "a", 5)
```

**Result:**
```
a!map(a: 5, b: 2)
```

label: append
documentation:
Appends a value or values to the given array, and returns the resulting array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
append([array], [value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
append({10, 20, 30}, 99)
```

**Result:**
```
{10, 20, 30, 99}
```

label: index
documentation:
Returns the data[index] if it is valid or else returns the default value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
index([data], [index], [default])
```

**Example:**
```appian
index({10, 20, 30}, 2, 1)
```

**Result:**
```
20
```

label: insert
documentation:
Inserts a value into the given array and returns the resulting array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
insert([array], [value], [index])
```

**Example:**
```appian
insert({10, 20, 30, 40}, 99, 1)
```

**Result:**
```
{99, 10, 20, 30, 40}
```

label: joinarray
documentation:
Concatenates the elements of an array together into one string and inserts a string separator between each element.

**Syntax:**
```appian
joinarray([array], [separator])
```

**Example:**
```appian
joinarray({1, 2, 3, 4}, "|")
```

**Result:**
```
1|2|3|4
```

label: ldrop
documentation:
Drops a given number of values from the left side of an array and returns the resulting array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
ldrop([array], [number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
ldrop({10, 20, 30}, 1)
```

**Result:**
```
{20, 30}
```

label: length
documentation:
This function returns the number of elements in an array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
length([array])
```

**Example:**
```appian
length({10, 20, 30})
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: rdrop
documentation:
Drops a given number of values from the right side of an array, and returns the resulting array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
rdrop([array], [number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
rdrop({10, 20, 30}, 1)
```

**Result:**
```
{10, 20}
```

label: remove
documentation:
Removes the value at a given index from an array, and returns the resulting array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
remove([array], [index])
```

**Example:**
```appian
remove({10, 20, 30}, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
{10, 30}
```

label: reverse
documentation:
Returns an array in reverse order.

**Syntax:**
```appian
reverse([array])
```

**Example:**
```appian
reverse({10, 20, 30})
```

**Result:**
```
{30, 20, 10}
```

label: updatearray
documentation:
Inserts new values or modifies existing values at the specified index of a given array, and returns the resulting array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
updatearray([array], [index], [value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
updatearray({10, 20, 30}, 2, 99)
```

**Result:**
```
{10, 99, 30}
```

label: where
documentation:
Returns the indexes where the values in the input array are true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
where([booleanArray], [default])
```

**Example:**
```appian
where({true, false, true})
```

**Result:**
```
{1, 3}
```

label: wherecontains
documentation:
Receives one or more values and returns an array of indexes that indicate the position of the values within the array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
wherecontains([values], [array])
```

**Example:**
```appian
wherecontains(20, {10, 20, 30})
```

**Result:**
```
{2}
```

label: bin2dec
documentation:
Converts a Binary number as text to a Decimal number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
bin2dec([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
bin2dec(10000)
```

**Result:**
```
16
```

label: bin2hex
documentation:
Converts a Binary number as text to a Hex number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
bin2hex([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
bin2hex(1100100)
```

**Result:**
```
64
```

label: bin2oct
documentation:
Converts a Binary number as text to an Octal number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
bin2oct([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
bin2oct(1100100)
```

**Result:**
```
144
```

label: dec2bin
documentation:
Converts a Decimal number to a Binary number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
dec2bin([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
dec2bin(16)
```

**Result:**
```
10000
```

label: dec2hex
documentation:
Converts a Decimal number to a Hex number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
dec2hex([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
dec2hex(16)
```

**Result:**
```
10
```

label: dec2oct
documentation:
Converts a Decimal number to an Octal number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
dec2oct([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
dec2oct(16)
```

**Result:**
```
20
```

label: hex2bin
documentation:
Converts a Hex number as text to a Binary number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
hex2bin([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
hex2bin(64)
```

**Result:**
```
1100100
```

label: hex2dec
documentation:
Converts a Hex number as text to a Decimal number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
hex2dec([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
hex2dec(10)
```

**Result:**
```
16
```

label: hex2oct
documentation:
Converts a Hex number as text to an Octal number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
hex2oct([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
hex2oct(64)
```

**Result:**
```
144
```

label: oct2bin
documentation:
Converts an Octal number as text to a Binary number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
oct2bin([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
oct2bin(144)
```

**Result:**
```
1100100
```

label: oct2dec
documentation:
Converts an Octal number as text to a Decimal number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
oct2dec([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
oct2dec(20)
```

**Result:**
```
16
```

label: oct2hex
documentation:
Converts an Octal number as text to a Hex number as text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
oct2hex([value], [place])
```

**Example:**
```appian
oct2hex(144)
```

**Result:**
```
64
```

label: a!chatMessage
documentation:
Creates a ChatMessage object for use in the messages parameter of a!chatField.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!chatMessage([messageContent], [role], [showWhen])
```

label: a!httpAuthenticationBasic
documentation:
Creates an object that contains the information required to perform HTTP Basic authentication.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!httpAuthenticationBasic([username], [password], [preemptive])
```

label: a!httpFormPart
documentation:
Creates an HTTP form part which can be passed in an integration’s multipart request body.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!httpFormPart([name], [contentType], [value])
```

label: a!httpHeader
documentation:
Creates an HTTP header object which can be passed to an HTTP function.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!httpHeader([name], [value])
```

label: a!httpQueryParameter
documentation:
Creates an HTTP query parameter object which can be passed to an HTTP function.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!httpQueryParameter([name], [value])
```

label: a!scsField
documentation:
Creates an object which contains the information required to access data in the Secure Credentials Store.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!scsField([externalSystemKey], [fieldKey], [usePerUser])
```

label: a!verifyRecaptcha
documentation:
Allows you to verify the reCAPTCHA connection was successful and access reCAPTCHA scores to help protect your Portal against potentially malicious activity.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!verifyRecaptcha([onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!wsConfig
documentation:
Constructs the config parameter to the webservicequery() and webservicewrite() functions.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!wsConfig([wsdlUrl], [service], [port], [operation], [wsdlCredentials], [endpointcredentials], [extensions])
```

label: a!wsHttpCredentials
documentation:
Constructs a WsHttpCredentials object for use with a!wsConfig.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!wsHttpCredentials([username], [password], [domain])
```

label: a!wsHttpHeaderField
documentation:
Constructs a WsHttpHeaderField object for use with a!wsConfig.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!wsHttpHeaderField([name], [name])
```

label: a!wsUsernameToken
documentation:
Constructs a WsUsernameToken object for use with a!wsConfig.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!wsUsernameToken([username], [password])
```

label: a!wsUsernameTokenScs
documentation:
Constructs a WsUsernameTokenScs object for use with a!wsConfig.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!wsUsernameTokenScs([systemKey], [usePerUser])
```

label: displayvalue
documentation:
Tries to match a value in a given array with a value at the same index in a replacement array and returns either the value at the same index or a default value if the value is not found.

**Syntax:**
```appian
displayvalue([value], [inArray], [replacement], [default])
```

**Example:**
```appian
displayvalue( 1, {0, 1, 2}, {"Low", "Medium", "High"}, "Unknown" )
```

**Result:**
```
Medium
```

label: externalize
documentation:
Converts the given value to a string representation so that it can be saved externally.

**Syntax:**
```appian
externalize([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
externalize(todocument(1))
```

label: internalize
documentation:
Converts the given externalized string representation of a value to the original value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
internalize([externalizedText], [default])
```

**Example:**
```appian
internalize(externalize(todocument(1)))
```

**Result:**
```
[Document:1]
```

label: toboolean
documentation:
Converts a value to Boolean.

**Syntax:**
```appian
toboolean([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
toboolean(0)
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: tocommunity
documentation:
Converts a value to Community.

**Syntax:**
```appian
tocommunity([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
tocommunity(1)
```

**Result:**
```
[Community:1]
```

label: todate
documentation:
Converts a value to Date with Timezone.

**Syntax:**
```appian
todate([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
todate(0)
```

**Result:**
```
1/1/2035
```

label: todatetime
documentation:
Converts a value to Date and Time with Timezone.

**Syntax:**
```appian
todatetime([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
todatetime(date(2005, 12, 13))
```

**Result:**
```
12/13/05 12:00 AM GMT
```

label: todecimal
documentation:
Converts a value to Decimal (double-precision floating-point number).

**Syntax:**
```appian
todecimal([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
todecimal("3.6")
```

**Result:**
```
3.6
```

label: todocument
documentation:
Converts a value to Document.

**Syntax:**
```appian
todocument([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
todocument(1)
```

**Result:**
```
[Document:1]
```

label: toemailaddress
documentation:
Converts a value to email address.

**Syntax:**
```appian
toemailaddress([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
toemailaddress("john.doe"&char(64)&"company.com")
```

**Result:**
```
john.doe@company.com
```

label: toemailrecipient
documentation:
Converts a value to email recipient.

**Syntax:**
```appian
toemailrecipient([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
toemailrecipient(togroup(1))
```

**Result:**
```
[Group:1]
```

label: tofolder
documentation:
Converts a value to Folder.

**Syntax:**
```appian
tofolder([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
tofolder({"1","2"})
```

**Result:**
```
[Folder:1], [Folder:2]
```

label: tointeger
documentation:
Converts a value to Integer.

**Syntax:**
```appian
tointeger([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
tointeger("3")
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: tointervalds
documentation:
Converts a value to Interval (Day to Second).

**Syntax:**
```appian
tointervalds([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
tointervalds("11h 10m 30s")
```

**Result:**
```
111030::00:00:00.000
```

label: toknowledgecenter
documentation:
Converts a value to Knowledge Center.

**Syntax:**
```appian
toknowledgecenter([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
toknowledgecenter("2")
```

**Result:**
```
[Knowledge Center:2]
```

label: tostring
documentation:
Converts a value to Text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
tostring([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
tostring(17)
```

**Result:**
```
"17"
```

label: totime
documentation:
Converts a value to Time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
totime([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
totime(datetime(2005, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0))
```

**Result:**
```
12:00 PM GMT
```

label: touniformstring
documentation:
Converts a value or list to text, preserving the original scalar or array structure.

**Syntax:**
```appian
touniformstring([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
touniformstring("John Doe 1060 West Addison Chicago", "IL")
```

**Result:**
```
{"John Doe 1060 West Addison Chicago", "IL"}
```

label: a!customFieldConcat
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function concatenates the specified values into a single value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldConcat([value])
```

label: a!customFieldCondition
documentation:
Used in the whenTrue parameter of a!customFieldMatch(), this function allows you to create a condition.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldCondition([field], [operator], [value])
```

label: a!customFieldDateDiff
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function returns the difference between two dates as a Number (Integer). The difference can be returned in days, hours, minutes, or seconds. Returns null when the startDate or endDate is null or empty.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldDateDiff([startDate], [endDate], [interval])
```

label: a!customFieldDefaultValue
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function returns a default value when the specified value is null or empty. All parameters must be of the same data type. When there are multiple default parameters, each parameter is evaluated in order and the first non-null or non-empty default will be returned.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldDefaultValue([value], [default])
```

label: a!customFieldDivide
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function returns the results of dividing two numbers. You can use record fields, related record fields, or literal values of type Number (Integer) or Number (Decimal) in your calculation.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldDivide([numerator], [denominator])
```

label: a!customFieldLogicalExpression
documentation:
Used in the whenTrue parameter of a!customFieldMatch(), this function allows you to group multiple logical conditions using the "AND" and "OR" operators.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldLogicalExpression([operator], [conditions])
```

label: a!customFieldMatch
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function evaluates the value parameter against multiple conditions and returns a value based on a match. If no match is found, the default parameter is returned.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldMatch([value], [equals], [whenTrue], [then], [default])
```

label: a!customFieldMultiply
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function returns the result of multiplying a series of values. You can multiply record fields, related record fields, or literal values of type Number (Integer) or Number (Decimal).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldMultiply([value])
```

label: a!customFieldSubtract
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function returns the difference between two numbers. You can subtract record fields, related record fields, or literal values of type Number (Integer) or Number (Decimal).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldSubtract([value1], [value2])
```

label: a!customFieldSum
documentation:
Used to create a real-time custom record field, this function returns a sum of values. You can calculate the sum of record fields, related record fields, or literal values of type Number (Integer) or Number (Decimal).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!customFieldSum([value])
```

label: a!addDateTime
documentation:
Adds the specified increments of time to the startDateTime and returns a date and time value. You can select a process calendar to ensure the return value falls within the specified working days and time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!addDateTime([startDateTime], [years], [months], [days], [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [useProcessCalendar], [processCalendarName])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!addDateTime(startDateTime: datetime(2000,1,1,0,0,0), months: 1, days: 1, hours: 1, minutes: 1, seconds: 1)
```

**Result:**
```
2/2/2000 1:01 AM GMT+00:00
```
example: a!addDateTime(startDateTime: datetime(2000,1,1,0,0,0), months: 1, days: 1, hours: 1, minutes: 1, seconds: 1)

label: calisworkday
documentation:
This returns whether or not the given Date and Time is a work day, according to the calendar defined for the system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
calisworkday([datetime], [calendar_name])
```

**Example:**
```appian
calisworkday(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0))
```

**Result:**
```
true
```

label: calisworktime
documentation:
This returns whether or not the given Date and Time is within working hours, according to the calendars defined for the system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
calisworktime([datetime], [calendar_name])
```

**Example:**
```appian
calisworktime(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0))
```

**Result:**
```
true
```

label: calworkdays
documentation:
This returns the actual number of work days between two Date and Times (both inclusive), according to the calendar defined for the system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
calworkdays([start_datetime], [end_datetime], [calendar_name])
```

**Example:**
```appian
calworkdays(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0), datetime(2011, 12, 20, 12, 0, 0))
```

**Result:**
```
6
```

label: calworkhours
documentation:
This returns the actual number of work hours between two given Date and Times (both inclusive), according to the calendar defined for the system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
calworkhours([start_datetime], [end_datetime], [calendar_name])
```

**Example:**
```appian
calworkhours(datetime(2011, 12, 12, 12, 0, 0), datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0))
```

**Result:**
```
8
```

label: date
documentation:
Converts text into data accepted by the date data type and functions that require date parameters.

**Syntax:**
```appian
date([year], [month], [day])
```

**Example:**
```appian
date(2011, 12, 13)
```

**Result:**
```
12/13/2011
```

label: datetime
documentation:
Converts the given Date and Time into a serial number that holds the Date and Time data type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
datetime([year], [month], [day], [hour], [minute], [second])
```

**Example:**
```appian
datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0)
```

**Result:**
```
12/13/2011 12:00 PM GMT
```

label: datevalue
documentation:
Converts a value to a date.

**Syntax:**
```appian
datevalue([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
datevalue(0)
```

**Result:**
```
1/1/2035
```

label: day
documentation:
Returns the day of the month from the date specified.

**Syntax:**
```appian
day([date])
```

**Example:**
```appian
day(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0))
```

**Result:**
```
13
```

label: dayofyear
documentation:
Returns the day number within the year.

**Syntax:**
```appian
dayofyear([date])
```

**Example:**
```appian
dayofyear(date(1957, 03, 14))
```

**Result:**
```
73
```

label: days360
documentation:
Returns the number of days between two dates, based on a 360-day calendar.

**Syntax:**
```appian
days360([start_date], [end_date], [method])
```

**Example:**
```appian
days360(today(), today() + 365, 0)
```

**Result:**
```
360
```

label: daysinmonth
documentation:
Returns the number of days in the given month in the given year.

**Syntax:**
```appian
daysinmonth([month], [year])
```

**Example:**
```appian
daysinmonth(2, 1800)
```

**Result:**
```
28
```

label: edate
documentation:
Returns the date that is the number of months before or after the given starting date.

**Syntax:**
```appian
edate([starting_date], [months])
```

**Example:**
```appian
edate(date(2011, 12, 13), -6)
```

**Result:**
```
6/13/11
```

label: eomonth
documentation:
Returns the date for the last day of the month that is the number of months before or after the given starting date.

**Syntax:**
```appian
eomonth([starting_date], [months])
```

**Example:**
```appian
eomonth(date(2011, 12, 13), -6)
```

**Result:**
```
6/30/2011
```

label: gmt
documentation:
Subtracts a time zone offset from a given Date and Time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
gmt([datetime], [timezone])
```

**Example:**
```appian
gmt(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 05), "America/New_York")
```

**Result:**
```
12/13/2011 5:05 PM GMT
```

label: hour
documentation:
Returns the hour from the time specified.

**Syntax:**
```appian
hour([time])
```

**Example:**
```appian
hour(time(14, 20, 23))
```

**Result:**
```
14
```

label: intervalds
documentation:
Converts the given time components into an equivalent time duration, an interval expressing days to seconds. This value is treated as a duration (Joe ran the marathon in 3 hours and 23 minutes), not a point in time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
intervalds([hour], [minute], [second])
```

**Example:**
```appian
intervalds(2, 4, 5)
```

**Result:**
```
0::02:04:05.000
```

label: isleapyear
documentation:
Returns a Boolean value for whether the given year is a leap year.

**Syntax:**
```appian
isleapyear([year])
```

**Example:**
```appian
isleapyear(1996)
```

**Result:**
```
True
```

label: lastndays
documentation:
Returns a Boolean value for whether the given date is within the last given number of days.

**Syntax:**
```appian
lastndays([date], [n])
```

**Example:**
```appian
lastndays(date(2011, 12, 13), 6)
```

**Result:**
```
False
```

label: local
documentation:
This is a Date and Time addition function, adding time zone offset to given Date and Time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
local([datetime], [timezone])
```

**Example:**
```appian
local(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 05), "America/New_York")
```

**Result:**
```
12/13/2011 7:05 AM GMT
```

label: milli
documentation:
This function returns the millisecond portion of a timestamp or the decimal number that represents 1 millisecond in days.

**Syntax:**
```appian
milli([time])
```

**Example:**
```appian
milli(datetime(2011, 12, 13, 12, 0, 0, 25))
```

**Result:**
```
25
```

label: minute
documentation:
Returns the minute from the time specified.

**Syntax:**
```appian
minute([time], [minute])
```

**Example:**
```appian
minute(time(14, 20, 23))
```

**Result:**
```
20
```

label: month
documentation:
Returns the month from the specified date.

**Syntax:**
```appian
month([date])
```

**Example:**
```appian
month(date(2011, 12, 13))
```

**Result:**
```
12
```

label: networkdays
documentation:
Returns the number of working days between two specified dates.

**Syntax:**
```appian
networkdays([starting_date], [ending_date], [holidays])
```

**Example:**
```appian
networkdays(date(2011, 12, 13), date(2011, 12, 20))
```

**Result:**
```
6
```

label: now
documentation:
Returns the current Date and Time as a serial number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
now([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
now()
```

**Result:**
```
2/2/2022 2:02 PM
```

label: second
documentation:
Returns the seconds from the specified time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
second([time])
```

**Example:**
```appian
second(time(14, 20, 23))
```

**Result:**
```
23
```

label: a!subtractDateTime
documentation:
Subtracts the specified increments of time from the startDateTime and returns a date and time value. You can select a process calendar to ensure the return value falls within the specified working days and time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!subtractDateTime([startDateTime], [years], [months], [days], [hours], [minutes], [seconds], [useProcessCalendar], [processCalendarName])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!subtractDateTime(startDateTime: datetime(2001, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0), months: 1, days: 1, hours: 1, minutes: 1, seconds: 1)
```

**Result:**
```
11/29/2000 10:58 PM GMT+00:00
```
example: a!subtractDateTime(startDateTime: datetime(2001, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0), months: 1, days: 1, hours: 1, minutes: 1, seconds: 1)

label: time
documentation:
Converts the given time into an equivalent time value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
time([hour], [minute], [second], [millisecond])
```

**Example:**
```appian
time(14, 20, 23)
```

**Result:**
```
2:20 PM
```

label: timevalue
documentation:
Converts the given time into an equivalent interval.

**Syntax:**
```appian
timevalue([time_text])
```

**Example:**
```appian
timevalue(time(14, 20, 23))
```

**Result:**
```
2:20 PM
```

label: timezone
documentation:
Returns the default offset in minutes from GMT, which is generally the process initiator's time zone.

**Syntax:**
```appian
timezone([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
timezone()
```

**Result:**
```
0
```

label: timezoneid
documentation:
Returns the time zone ID for the current context.

**Syntax:**
```appian
timezoneid([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
timezoneid()
```

**Result:**
```
GMT
```

label: today
documentation:
Returns the current day in GMT.

**Syntax:**
```appian
today([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
today()
```

**Result:**
```
2/2/2022
```

label: weekday
documentation:
Returns the day of the week of the specified date.

**Syntax:**
```appian
weekday([date], [return_type])
```

**Example:**
```appian
weekday(date(2011, 12, 13))
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: weeknum
documentation:
Returns the week number within the year for the given date using a given methodology.

**Syntax:**
```appian
weeknum([date], [methodology])
```

**Example:**
```appian
weeknum(date(2011, 12, 13))
```

**Result:**
```
51
```

label: workday
documentation:
Returns the date the given number of workdays before or after the given date.

**Syntax:**
```appian
workday([starting_date], [days], [holidays])
```

**Example:**
```appian
workday(date(2011, 12, 13), -6)
```

**Result:**
```
12/5/2011
```

label: year
documentation:
Returns the year for the date specified.

**Syntax:**
```appian
year([date])
```

**Example:**
```appian
year(date(2011, 12, 13))
```

**Result:**
```
2011
```

label: yearfrac
documentation:
Determine the fraction of the year.

**Syntax:**
```appian
yearfrac([start_date], [end_date], [method])
```

**Example:**
```appian
yearfrac(today(), today() + 270, 3)
```

**Result:**
```
0.739726
```

label: a!asyncVariable
documentation:
Use in a!localVariables() to load data asynchronously and configure refresh behavior for local variables that take more than 500 ms to load. Components that depend on the local variable will show a placeholder until the data is ready. To control the refresh behavior without loading the data asynchronously, use a!refreshVariable(). When used outside of an interface, the refresh parameters are ignored and the variable doesn’t load asynchronously.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!asyncVariable([value], [refreshOnReferencedVarChange], [refreshOnVarChange], [refreshAfter])
```

label: a!controlPanelRecords
documentation:
Function that either creates a map or returns the data for an individual record in the base record type for a control panel.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!controlPanelRecords([controlPanel], [record])
```

label: a!localVariables
documentation:
Lets you define one or more local variables for use within an expression. When used within an interface, the value of each variable can be refreshed under a variety of conditions, configured using a!refreshVariable(). When used outside of an interface, all refresh properties configured using a!refreshVariable() are ignored.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!localVariables([localVar1], [localVarN], [expression])
```

label: a!refreshVariable
documentation:
The configuration for a specific local variable for use within a!localVariables(). When used within an interface, the value of the variable can be refreshed under a variety of conditions. When used outside of an interface, all refresh properties are ignored.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!refreshVariable([value], [refreshAlways], [refreshInterval], [refreshOnReferencedVarChange], [refreshOnVarChange], [refreshAfter])
```

label: bind
documentation:
Use in conjunction with the load function to bind getter and setter functions to a variable. When the variable is read, the getter function or rule will be called. When the variable is saved into, the writer returned by the setter function or rule will be called. The setter function must return a writer.

**Syntax:**
```appian
bind([get], [set])
```

label: load
documentation:
Lets you define local variables within an expression for an interface and evaluate the expression with the new variables, then re-evaluate the function with the local variables' values from the previous evaluation.

**Syntax:**
```appian
load([localVar1], [localVarN], [expression])
```

label: a!save
documentation:
In interface saveInto parameters, updates the target with the given value. Use a!save for each item that you want to modify or alter in a saveInto parameter. This function has no effect when called outside of a component's saveInto parameter.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!save([target], [value])
```

label: with
documentation:
Lets you define local variables within a function and evaluate the expression with the new variables.

**Syntax:**
```appian
with([localVar1], [localVarN], [expression])
```

**Example:**
```appian
with(local!a:10, local!b:20, local!a+local!b)
```

**Result:**
```
30
```

label: a!automationId
documentation:
Returns the automation identifier for the automation type provided. Use this function to write the automation identifier for record events.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!automationId([automationTypes])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!automationId("RPA")
```

**Result:**
```
2
```

label: a!automationType
documentation:
Returns the automation type for the automation identifier provided, translated according to the user’s language preferences.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!automationType([automationIds])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!automationType(1)
```

**Result:**
```
None (User)
```

label: a!defaultValue
documentation:
Returns a default value when the specified value is null or empty. When there are multiple default parameters, each parameter is evaluated in order and the first non-null and non-empty default will be returned. Null, "", and {} are all considered null or empty values.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!defaultValue([value], [default])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!defaultValue( null, date(2021,10,1), null)
```

**Result:**
```
`2021-10-01`
```

label: a!keys
documentation:
Returns the keys of the provided map, dictionary, CDT, or record.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!keys([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!keys(value: a!map(a: 1, b: 2))
```

**Result:**
```
{"a", "b"}
```

label: a!listType
documentation:
Returns the list type number for a given type number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!listType([typeNumber])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!listType(typeof(1))
```

label: cast
documentation:
Converts a value from its existing type to the specified type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
cast([typeNumber], [value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
cast(type!Date,"01-01-2017")
```

**Result:**
```
1/1/2017
```

label: error
documentation:
Raises an error with the given message, used for invalidating execution.This function never returns a value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
error([message])
```

**Example:**
```appian
error("This is an error message.")
```

**Result:**
```
Expression evaluation error at function 'error': This is an error message.
```
exampleResult: Expression evaluation error at function 'error': This is an error message.

label: infinity
documentation:
Represents a constant number that stands for positive infinity or a negative infinity if you negate the value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
infinity([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
infinity()
```

**Result:**
```
∞
```

label: isinfinite
documentation:
Tests given numbers against positive and negative infinity, returning true if the number is infinite, false if the number is not infinite.

**Syntax:**
```appian
isinfinite([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
isinfinite(1.5, 1.1)
```

**Result:**
```
false; false
```

label: isnegativeinfinity
documentation:
Tests given numbers against negative infinity, returning true if number is negative infinity, false if number is not negative infinity.

**Syntax:**
```appian
isnegativeinfinity([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
isnegativeinfinity(1)
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: a!isNotNullOrEmpty
documentation:
Returns false if the value is null, an empty string, or an empty list. Otherwise returns true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!isNotNullOrEmpty([value])
```

label: isnull
documentation:
Returns true if value is null, false otherwise.

**Syntax:**
```appian
isnull([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
isnull("")
```

**Result:**
```
true
```

label: a!isNullOrEmpty
documentation:
Returns true if the value is null, an empty string, or an empty list. Otherwise returns false.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!isNullOrEmpty([value])
```

label: ispositiveinfinity
documentation:
Tests given numbers against positive infinity, returning true if the numbers are positive infinity, false if the numbers are not positive infinity.

**Syntax:**
```appian
ispositiveinfinity([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
ispositiveinfinity(1)
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: nan
documentation:
Constant number representing Not A Number, generally used for comparison to the result of mathematical operations with invalid inputs. This is equivalent to a decimal (floating point) null, but nan() is provided for more explicit usage in mathematical expressions.

**Syntax:**
```appian
nan([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
nan()
```

**Result:**
```
null
```

label: null
documentation:
Returns a null value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
null([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
null()
```

**Result:**
```
null
```

label: runtimetypeof
documentation:
Returns the numerical representation of an Appian system data type when used during process execution.

**Syntax:**
```appian
runtimetypeof([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
runtimetypeof( 12345)
```

**Result:**
```
9
```

label: a!submittedOfflineTaskIds
documentation:
In an offline interface in Appian Mobile, returns a list of offline tasks ids that the user has submitted since their last data sync. If evaluated anywhere else, returns an empty list. Use this to automatically remove tasks from an offline task report after a user submits them.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!submittedOfflineTaskIds([])
```

label: typename
documentation:
Returns the type name of a given type number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
typename([typeNumber])
```

**Example:**
```appian
typename(typeof(1))
```

**Result:**
```
Number (Integer)
```

label: typeof
documentation:
Returns the type number of a given value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
typeof([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
typeof("a")
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: and
documentation:
Returns true if all inputs are true; returns false if at least one input is false.

**Syntax:**
```appian
and([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
and(true(), false())
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: choose
documentation:
Evaluates the choice argument at the given index and returns the result.

**Syntax:**
```appian
choose([key], [choice1], [choiceN])
```

**Example:**
```appian
choose(2, "a", "b", "c")
```

**Result:**
```
b
```

label: false
documentation:
Returns the Boolean value false.

**Syntax:**
```appian
false([returns])
```

**Example:**
```appian
false()
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: if
documentation:
Returns valueIfTrue if condition returns true; returns valueIfFalse otherwise.

**Syntax:**
```appian
if([condition], [valueIfTrue], [valueIfFalse])
```

**Example:**
```appian
if(isleapyear(2021), 1, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
2
```

label: a!match
documentation:
Evaluates the value against multiple conditions and returns a value based on a match. If no match is found, the default is returned. For example, if "a" then "b" else "c".

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!match([value], [equals], [whenTrue], [then], [default])
```

label: not
documentation:
Converts true into false, and false into true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
not([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
not(true(), false())
```

**Result:**
```
false, true
```

label: or
documentation:
Returns true if any inputs are true; returns false if all inputs are false.

**Syntax:**
```appian
or([value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
or(true(), false())
```

**Result:**
```
true
```

label: true
documentation:
Returns the Boolean value true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
true([returns])
```

**Example:**
```appian
true()
```

**Result:**
```
true
```

label: a!forEach
documentation:
Evaluates an expression for each item in a list and returns a new array of the results.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!forEach([items], [expression])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!forEach(items: {1, 2, 3}, expression: fv!item + 10)
```

**Result:**
```
{11, 12, 13}
```

label: all
documentation:
Calls a rule or function that returns either true or false for each item in list, asks the question, "Do all items in this list yield true for this rule/function?", and returns true if all items in list evaluates to true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
all([predicate], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
all(fn!isnull, {10, null, 30})
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: any
documentation:
Calls a rule or function that returns either true or false for each item in list by asking the question, "Do any items in this list yield true for this rule/function?" with the intent to discover if any item(s) yield true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
any([predicate], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
any(fn!isnull, {10, null, 30})
```

**Result:**
```
true
```

label: apply
documentation:
Calls a rule or function for each item in a list, and provides any contexts specified.

**Syntax:**
```appian
apply([function], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
apply(fn!sum, {-1, 2, 3}, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
{1, 4, 5}
```

label: filter
documentation:
Calls a predicate for each item in a list and returns any items for which the returned value is true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
filter([predicate], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
filter(fn!isleapyear, {2015, 2016, 2017})
```

**Result:**
```
{2016}
```

label: merge
documentation:
Takes a variable number of lists and merges them into a single list (or a list of lists) that is the size of the largest list provided.

**Syntax:**
```appian
merge([list])
```

**Example:**
```appian
merge({10, 20}, {30, 40})
```

**Result:**
```
{{10, 30}, {20, 40}}
```

label: none
documentation:
Calls a rule or function that returns either true or false for each item in list by asking the question, "Do all items in this list yield false for this rule/function?" with the intent to discover if no items will yield true.

**Syntax:**
```appian
none([predicate], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
none(fn!isnull, {1, null, 3})
```

**Result:**
```
false
```

label: reduce
documentation:
Calls a rule or function for each item in a list, passing the result of each call to the next one, and returns the value of the last computation.

**Syntax:**
```appian
reduce([function], [initial], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
reduce(fn!sum, 1, {2, 3, 4})
```

**Result:**
```
10
```

label: reject
documentation:
Calls a predicate for each item in a list, rejects any items for which the returned value is true, and returns all remaining items.

**Syntax:**
```appian
reject([predicate], [list], [context])
```

**Example:**
```appian
reject(fn!isnull, {1, null, 3})
```

**Result:**
```
{1, 3}
```

label: abs
documentation:
Returns the absolute value(s) of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
abs([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
abs(-1)
```

**Result:**
```
1
```

label: ceiling
documentation:
Rounds the number up to the nearest multiple of the specified significance.

**Syntax:**
```appian
ceiling([number], [significance])
```

**Example:**
```appian
ceiling(1.6)
```

**Result:**
```
2
```

label: combin
documentation:
Calculates the number of unique ways to choose m elements from a pool of n elements.

**Syntax:**
```appian
combin([n], [m])
```

**Example:**
```appian
combin(4, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
6
```

label: a!distanceBetween
documentation:
Returns the distance between the two locations (in meters) specified by the start and end coordinates. The distance is calculated by tracing a line between the two locations that follows the curvature of the Earth, and measuring the length of the resulting arc.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!distanceBetween([startLatitude], [startLongitude], [endLatitude], [endLongitude])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!distanceBetween(startLatitude: 38.932290, startLongitude: -77.218490, endLatitude: 38.963058, endLongitude: -77.363701)
```

**Result:**
```
13015.34
```

label: e
documentation:
Returns the value of e.

**Syntax:**
```appian
e([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
e()
```

**Result:**
```
2.718282
```

label: enumerate
documentation:
Returns a list of integer numbers from 0 through n-1.

**Syntax:**
```appian
enumerate([n])
```

**Example:**
```appian
enumerate(5)
```

**Result:**
```
0, 1, 2, 3, 4
```

label: even
documentation:
Rounds positive numbers up to nearest even integer and negative numbers down to the nearest even integer.

**Syntax:**
```appian
even([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
even(5)
```

**Result:**
```
6
```

label: exp
documentation:
Returns e raised to the specified power.

**Syntax:**
```appian
exp([power])
```

**Example:**
```appian
exp(2)
```

**Result:**
```
7.389056
```

label: fact
documentation:
The factorial of specified number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
fact([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
fact(4)
```

**Result:**
```
24
```

label: factdouble
documentation:
The double factorial of specified number (mathematically n!!).

**Syntax:**
```appian
factdouble([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
factdouble(3)
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: floor
documentation:
Rounds the number down to the nearest multiple of the specified significance.

**Syntax:**
```appian
floor([number], [significance])
```

**Example:**
```appian
floor(2.8888, .01)
```

**Result:**
```
2.88
```

label: int
documentation:
Rounds the specified number down to the nearest integer.

**Syntax:**
```appian
int([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
int(2.8888)
```

**Result:**
```
2
```

label: ln
documentation:
Returns the natural logarithm of the specified number, which is the power that e must be raised to in order to equal the specified number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
ln([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
ln(e())
```

**Result:**
```
1
```

label: log
documentation:
Returns the logarithm of the number using the specified base, which is the power that base must be raised to, to equal the number.

**Syntax:**
```appian
log([number], [base])
```

**Example:**
```appian
log(25, 5)
```

**Result:**
```
2
```

label: mod
documentation:
Returns the remainder of dividend when divided by the divisor.

**Syntax:**
```appian
mod([dividend], [divisor])
```

**Example:**
```appian
mod(40, 15)
```

**Result:**
```
10
```

label: mround
documentation:
Rounds the number to the specified multiple.

**Syntax:**
```appian
mround([number], [multiple])
```

**Example:**
```appian
mround(2.8888, .01)
```

**Result:**
```
2.89
```

label: multinomial
documentation:
Adds the specified integers and divides the factorial of the sum by the factorial of the individual numbers.

**Syntax:**
```appian
multinomial([integer])
```

**Example:**
```appian
multinomial(1, 2, 3)
```

**Result:**
```
60
```

label: odd
documentation:
Rounds positive numbers up to nearest odd integer and negative numbers down to the nearest odd integer.

**Syntax:**
```appian
odd([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
odd(10)
```

**Result:**
```
11
```

label: pi
documentation:
Returns the value of pi.

**Syntax:**
```appian
pi([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
pi()
```

**Result:**
```
3.141593
```

label: power
documentation:
Returns the base number raised to the specified exponent.

**Syntax:**
```appian
power([base], [exponent])
```

**Example:**
```appian
power(10, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
100
```

label: product
documentation:
Returns the product of the specified numbers.

**Syntax:**
```appian
product([factor])
```

**Example:**
```appian
product(10, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
20
```

label: quotient
documentation:
Returns the quotient when numerator is divided by the denominator, and drops the remainder.

**Syntax:**
```appian
quotient([numerator], [denominator])
```

**Example:**
```appian
quotient(20, 10)
```

**Result:**
```
2
```

label: rand
documentation:
Returns a random number between 0 and 1 based on an even probability distribution, which is seeded by the transaction time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
rand([count])
```

**Example:**
```appian
rand()
```

**Result:**
```
0.4506349
```

label: round
documentation:
Rounds off the number to the specified number of digits.

**Syntax:**
```appian
round([number], [num_digits])
```

**Example:**
```appian
round(2.8888, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
2.89
```

label: rounddown
documentation:
Rounds the number down to the specified digit.

**Syntax:**
```appian
rounddown([number], [num_digits])
```

**Example:**
```appian
round(2.8888, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
2.88
```

label: roundup
documentation:
Rounds the number up to the specified digit.

**Syntax:**
```appian
roundup([number], [num_digits])
```

**Example:**
```appian
round(2.8888, 2)
```

**Result:**
```
2.89
```

label: sign
documentation:
Returns the number divided by its absolute value, which is 1 if the number is positive and -1 if the number is negative.

**Syntax:**
```appian
sign([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
sign(-10)
```

label: sqrt
documentation:
Returns the square root(s) of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
sqrt([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
sqrt(25)
```

**Result:**
```
5
```

label: sqrtpi
documentation:
Multiplies the number by pi, then returns the square root of the product.

**Syntax:**
```appian
sqrtpi([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
sqrtpi(25 / pi())
```

**Result:**
```
5
```

label: sum
documentation:
Returns the sum of the specified numbers.

**Syntax:**
```appian
sum([addend])
```

**Example:**

label: harmean
documentation:
Returns the harmonic mean of the specified number(s), which is the number of terms divided by the sum of the terms' reciprocals.

**Syntax:**
```appian
harmean([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
harmean(1, 2, 3)
```

**Result:**
```
1.636364
```

label: lcm
documentation:
Returns the least common multiple of the specified non-negative number(s), which is the smallest number that is a multiple of all the given numbers.

**Syntax:**
```appian
lcm([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
lcm(5,10,15)
```

**Result:**
```
30
```

label: lookup
documentation:
Returns location of data within multiple values, or valueIfNotPresent.

**Syntax:**
```appian
lookup([multipleValues], [dataToLookup], [valueIfNotPresent])
```

**Example:**
```appian
lookup({"a", "b", "c", "d"}, "c", -1)
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: max
documentation:
Returns the maximum of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
max([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
max(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
4
```

label: median
documentation:
Returns the median of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
median([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
median(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
2.5
```

label: min
documentation:
Returns the minimum of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
min([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
min(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
1
```

label: mode
documentation:
Returns the mode of the specified number(s), which is the most commonly repeated element.

**Syntax:**
```appian
mode([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
mode(1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: rank
documentation:
Returns an integer representing the rank of the number in the specified array.

**Syntax:**
```appian
rank([number], [array], [order])
```

**Example:**
```appian
rank(2, {1, 2, 3, 4}, false)
```

**Result:**
```
3
```

label: stdev
documentation:
Returns the standard deviation of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
stdev([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
stdev(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
1.290994
```

label: stdevp
documentation:
Returns the standard deviation of the specified number(s), assuming that the numbers form the entire data set and not just a sample.

**Syntax:**
```appian
stdevp([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
stdevp(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
1.118034
```

label: var
documentation:
Returns the variance of the specified number(s).

**Syntax:**
```appian
var([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
var(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
1.666667
```

label: varp
documentation:
Returns the variance of the specified number(s), assuming that the numbers form the entire data set and not just a sample.

**Syntax:**
```appian
varp([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
varp(1, 2, 3, 4)
```

**Result:**
```
1.25
```

label: a!applyValidations
documentation:
This function references pre-configured record field validations and allows you to create additional validations to apply. Additional validations can be used to address less common scenarios that aren't applicable to every use of a record field.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!applyValidations([recordField], [context], [additionalValidations])
```

label: a!applyComponents
documentation:
Calls a rule or function for each item in a list and supports the preservation of the local state on interfaces.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!applyComponents([function], [array], [arrayVariable])
```

label: a!controlPanelRecordHierarchyMetadata
documentation:
Returns relevant information for the specified category or type and its parent(s). This includes record fields and other information associated with the category or type, such as the display name, icon, and color.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!controlPanelRecordHierarchyMetadata([controlPanel], [recordUuid])
```

label: a!dataSubset
documentation:
Creates a value of type DataSubset for defining the source of expression-backed records and for use with a!pickerFieldCustom, leaving the data as provided. To apply sorting and paging, use todatasubset().

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!dataSubset([startIndex], [batchSize], [sort], [totalCount], [data], [identifiers])
```

label: a!deployment
documentation:
Returns a specific property of direct and external deployments.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deployment([deploymentUuid], [property])
```

**Example:**
```appian
`deployment(101, "reviewedDate")`
```

**Result:**
```
`12/21/05 2:28 PM GMT`
```

label: a!endsWith
documentation:
Checks if a text value ends with a specified text value. For example: endsWith("apple","e") returns TRUE.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!endsWith([text], [endsWithText])
```

label: a!entityData
documentation:
Creates an Entity Data for use with a!writeToMultipleDataStoreEntities()

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!entityData([entity], [data])
```

label: a!entityDataIdentifiers
documentation:
Creates an EntityDataIdentifiers configuration for use with a!deleteFromDataStoreEntities().

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!entityDataIdentifiers([entity], [identifiers])
```

label: a!executeStoredProcedureForQuery
documentation:
Executes a stored procedure in a database. Since this function could run more than once, do not use it with stored procedures that modify data to avoid unintentional changes.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!executeStoredProcedureForQuery([dataSource], [procedureName], [inputs], [timeout], [autoCommit])
```

label: a!fromJson
documentation:
Converts a JSON string into an Appian value.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!fromJson([jsonText])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!fromJson("{""name"":""John Smith"", ""age"":49, ""likes"":[""green"",""dogs"",""long walks""]}")
```

**Result:**
```
[name:John Smith,age:49,likes:green; dogs; long walks]
```

**Example:**

```appian
a!fromJson("{""name"":""John Smith"", ""age"":49, ""likes"":[""green"",""dogs"",""long walks""]}")
```

**Result:**

label: a!getDataSourceForPlugin
documentation:
Provides capability for plug-ins to connect to Data Source Connected Systems and apply corresponding role map security configurations.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!getDataSourceForPlugin([dataSourceConnectedSystem])
```

label: a!httpResponse
documentation:
Returns an HTTP Response object for use in a Web API.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!httpResponse([statusCode], [headers], [body])
```

label: a!httpResponse_17r4
documentation:

**Syntax:**

label: a!iconIndicator
documentation:
Returns the specified image from a list of standard indicator icons. Indicator icons can be used on interface within a document image.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!iconIndicator([icon])
```

label: a!iconNewsEvent
documentation:
Returns the specified image from a list of standard news event icons in one of six colors: blue, green, gray, orange, purple, or red.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!iconNewsEvent([icon], [color])
```

label: a!integrationError
documentation:
Creates an integration error value. Use when configuring custom error handling for integration objects.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!integrationError([title], [message], [detail])
```

label: a!isBetween
documentation:
Checks if a value is within the specified range. The value can be of type date, date and time, number integer, or number decimal.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!isBetween([value], [upperLimit], [lowerLimit])
```

label: a!isInText
documentation:
Checks if a text value contains a specified text value. For example: isInText("apple","p") returns TRUE.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!isInText([text], [subtext])
```

label: a!jsonPath
documentation:
Finds information in a JSON string. JSONPath is used to navigate through elements and attributes in a JSON string.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!jsonPath([value], [expression])
```

label: a!latestHealthCheck
documentation:
Returns the start time, run status, zip file, and report for the latest Health Check run.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!latestHealthCheck([])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!latestHealthCheck()
```

**Result:**
```
[startDateTime=04/25/2020 16:18:38 GMT+00:00, runStatus=COMPLETED, zip=[Document:1408], report=[Document:1409]]
```

label: a!listViewItem
documentation:
Creates a value of type ListViewItem for use with record type definitions.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!listViewItem([title], [details], [image], [timestamp])
```

label: a!pagingInfo
documentation:
Creates a value of type PagingInfo for use with grids, queries, and todatasubset().

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!pagingInfo([startIndex], [batchSize], [sort])
```

label: a!query
documentation:
Creates a Query object for use in the a!queryEntity() function.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!query([selection], [aggregation], [logicalExpression], [filter], [pagingInfo])
```

label: a!queryAggregation
documentation:

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryAggregation([aggregationColumns])
```

label: a!queryAggregationColumn
documentation:

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryAggregationColumn([field], [alias], [visible], [isGrouping], [aggregationFunction], [groupingFunction])
```

label: a!queryColumn
documentation:
Creates a Column object for use inside a Selection object.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryColumn([field], [alias], [visible])
```

label: a!queryEntity
documentation:
Executes a query on a given data store entity and returns the result.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryEntity([entity], [query], [fetchTotalCount])
```

label: a!queryEntity_18r3
documentation:
Executes a query on a given data store entity and returns the result.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryEntity_18r3([entity], [query])
```

label: a!queryEntity_22r2

Executes a query on a given data store entity and returns the result.

label: a!queryFilter
documentation:
Creates a value of type QueryFilter for use with a!pickerFieldRecords(), a!queryRecordType(), a!recordData(), a!relatedRecordData(), a!recordFilterListOption(), or a!query().

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryFilter([field], [operator], [value], [applyWhen])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!queryFilter(field: "name", operator: "=", value: 1)
```

**Result:**
```
[field=name, operator==, valueExpression=, value=1]
```

label: a!queryLogicalExpression
documentation:
Creates a LogicalExpression object that determines the filtration to apply in Query object.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryLogicalExpression([operator], [logicalExpressions], [filters], [ignoreFiltersWithEmptyValues])
```

label: a!queryProcessAnalytics
documentation:

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryProcessAnalytics([report], [query], [contextGroups], [contextProcessIds], [contextProcessModels], [contextUsers])
```

label: a!queryRecordByIdentifier
documentation:
Executes a query on a given record identifier and returns the record data.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!queryRecordByIdentifier([recordType], [identifier], [fields], [relatedRecordData])
```

label: a!querySelection
documentation:
Returns a Selection object for use inside a Query object.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!querySelection([columns])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!querySelection(columns: a!queryColumn(field: "name"))
```

**Result:**
```
[columns=[field=name, alias=name, visible=true]]
```

label: a!recordFilterChoices
documentation:
Creates choices of a user filter for a service-backed record type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!recordFilterChoices([choiceLabels], [choiceValues])
```

label: a!recordFilterDateRange
documentation:
Creates a user filter for a record list.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!recordFilterDateRange([name], [field], [isVisible], [defaultFrom], [defaultTo])
```

label: a!recordFilterDateRange_20r2
documentation:

**Syntax:**

```appian
a!recordFilterDateRange_20r2([name], [field], [defaultFrom], [defaultTo], [isVisible])
```

label: a!recordFilterList
documentation:


label: a!recordFilterListOption

**Syntax:**


label: a!sentimentScore
documentation:
Returns a list of scores representing the emotional or subjective sentiment expressed in each of the provided text values, ranging from 1.0 (positive) to -1.0 (negative).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!sentimentScore([text])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!sentimentScore({"Hi, I hope you're having a great day"})
```

**Result:**
```
{0.79}
```
example: a!sentimentScore({"Hi, I hope you're having a great day"})

label: a!sortInfo
documentation:
Creates a value of type SortInfo for use with grids and record queries.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!sortInfo([field], [ascending])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!sortInfo(field: "a", ascending: true())
```

**Result:**


label: a!startsWith

**Syntax:**

```appian
a!startsWith([text], [startsWithText])
```

label: a!storedProcedureInput
documentation:

**Syntax:**

```appian
a!storedProcedureInput([name], [value])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!storedProcedureInput(name: "integer_input", value: 2)
```

label: a!submitUploadedFiles
documentation:
To upload files outside of a start form or task, use this function in the saveInto parameter of a submit button or link. This function submits the files uploaded to any file upload or signature components in the interface to their target folder.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!submitUploadedFiles([onSuccess], [onError], [documents])
```

label: a!toJson
documentation:
Converts a value into a JSON string.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!toJson([value], [removeNullOrEmptyFields])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!toJson(a!pagingInfo(startIndex: 1, batchSize: 10))
```


label: a!toJson_17r1

**Syntax:**

```appian
a!callLanguageModel([prompt], [messages], [isStreaming])
```

label: a!documentFolderForRecordType

label: a!doesUserHaveAccess


label: a!map
documentation:
Creates a map of values (Any Type) with each value stored at the corresponding string key. Values stored in maps are not wrapped in variants.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!map([key1], [keyN])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!map(id: 1, name: "Jane Doe")
```

**Result:**
```
a!map(id: 1, name: "Jane Doe")
```

label: a!pageResponse

Identifies the data in the current integration response, and the path of the next set of data to request. This function should be used to return data in a record data source expression when the integration uses a non-integer pagination value.

**Syntax:**

```appian
a!pageResponse([data], [nextPage])
```

Executes a query on a given record type and returns the result. This is an older version of the a!queryRecordType() function, which has new functionality.

label: a!recordData
documentation:
This function references a set of records from a record type and allows additional filtering in a read-only grid, chart, or selection component that uses a record type as the source. When referencing one-to-many relationships in grid columns, you can filter, sort, and limit that related record set using the relatedRecordData parameter and the a!relatedRecordData() function.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!recordData([recordType], [filters], [relatedRecordData], [fields])
```

label: a!relatedRecordData
documentation:
References a one-to-many relationship defined on a record type and allows for additional filtering, sorting, and limiting of the related record set.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!relatedRecordData([relationship], [limit], [sort], [filters])
```

label: a!selectionFields
documentation:
Returns all fields from a record type and any of its related record types within a query. You can also query specific fields from other record types at the same time.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!selectionFields([allFieldsFromRecordType], [selectFields], [includeRealTimeCustomFields], [includeExtraLongTextFields])
```

label: a!testRunResultForId
documentation:
Provided a test-run ID, this function returns a TestRunResult data type containing the results of a rule test run. If the status of the test is IN PROGRESS, TestRunResult will contain only results for completed tests; if the status is COMPLETE, TestRunResult contains all test results.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!testRunResultForId([testRunId])
```

label: a!testRunStatusForId
documentation:
Provided a test-run ID, this function queries for the status of an expression rule test run.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!testRunStatusForId([testRunId])
```

label: cents
documentation:
Converts a number into its value in cents by effectively appending a cent sign to a fixed representation and one comma for every three digits preceding the decimal.

**Syntax:**
```appian
cents([number], [decimals])
```

**Example:**
```appian
cents( 123412 )
```

**Result:**
```
123,412.00¢
```

label: char
documentation:
Converts a number into its Unicode character equivalent.

**Syntax:**
```appian
char([number])
```

**Example:**
```appian
char( 65 )
```

**Result:**
```
A
```

label: charat
documentation:
Returns the character at given index within specified string.

**Syntax:**
```appian
charat([text], [index])
```

**Example:**
```appian
charat( "string", 2 )
```

**Result:**
```
t
```

label: clean
documentation:
Returns the specified text, minus any characters not considered printable. Printable characters are the 95 printable ASCII characters plus three special characters: BACKSPACE (0x08), TAB (0x09), and NEWLINE (0x0a).

**Syntax:**
```appian
clean([text])
```

**Example:**
```appian
clean( "Please enter value in £" )
```

**Result:**
```
Please enter value in
```

label: cleanwith
documentation:
Returns the specified text, minus any characters not in the list of valid characters.

**Syntax:**
```appian
cleanwith([text], [with])
```

**Example:**
```appian
cleanwith( "text string", "xte" )
```

**Result:**
```
textt
```

label: code
documentation:
Converts the text into Unicode integers.

**Syntax:**
```appian
code([text])
```

**Example:**
```appian
code( "Convert to Unicode" )
```

**Result:**
```
67, 111, 110, 118, 101, 114, 116, 32, 116, 111, 32, 85, 110, 105, 99, 111, 100, 101
```

label: concat
documentation:
Concatenates the specified strings into one string, without a separator.

**Syntax:**
```appian
concat([text])
```

**Example:**
```appian
concat( {"a", "b", "c"}, {"d", "e", "f"} )
```

**Result:**
```
abcdef
```

label: a!currency
documentation:
Localizes a currency value based on the given ISO currency code.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!currency([isoCode], [value], [decimalPlaces], [showSeparators], [format], [indicatorAlignment])
```

**Example:**
```appian
a!currency( "USD", 13213.43 )
```

**Result:**
```
$13,213.43
```

label: currency_22r4
documentation:
Converts a decimal number into a generic currency value by effectively adding a generic currency symbol (¤), a decimal point, and one comma for every three digits preceding the decimal.

**Syntax:**
```appian
currency_22r4([number], [decimals])
```

**Example:**
```appian
currency_22r4( 13213.43 )
```

**Result:**
```
¤13,213.43
```

label: exact
documentation:
Compares two given text strings in a case-sensitive manner, returning true only if they are exactly the same.

**Syntax:**
```appian
exact([text1], [text2])
```

**Example:**
```appian
exact( "Copy of the other", "Copy of the other" )
```

**Result:**
```
true
```
example: exact( "Copy of the other", "Copy of the other" )

label: extract
documentation:
Returns the value (or values, if the text contains multiple delimited values) between the delimiters from the given text.

**Syntax:**
```appian
extract([text], [startDelimiter], [endDelimiter])
```

**Example:**
```appian
extract( "name: Bob, name: Karen, name: Sam", ":", "," )
```

```appian
a!createUser([username], [firstName], [nickname], [middleName], [lastName], [email], [sendAccountCreationEmail], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!cancelProcess
documentation:
The Cancel Process Smart Service allows you to halt all process flow and cancel all tasks associated with a process.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!cancelProcess([processId], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!cancelProcess_17r3
documentation:
The Cancel Process Smart Service allows you to halt all process flow and cancel all tasks associated with a process.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!cancelProcess_17r3([processId], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!updateUserType
documentation:
The Change User Type Smart Service allows you to select a user and change the user's type from a Basic User to a System Administrator or from a System Administrator back to a Basic User.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!updateUserType([user], [newUserType], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!completeTask
documentation:
The Complete Task Smart Service allows you to complete a task belonging to another process from your current process.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!completeTask([taskId], [taskInputs], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!createFolder
documentation:
The Create Folder smart service allows you to create a folder to store files within the Appian file system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!createFolder([name], [parentFolder], [parentKnowledgeCenter], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!createGroup
documentation:
The Create Group smart service allows you to add new groups at runtime via process model.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!createGroup([name], [description], [groupType], [delegatedCreation], [parent], [membershipPolicy], [securityType], [groupPrivacy], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!createKnowledgeCenter
documentation:
A knowledge center is a second-level container in Appian Document Management (below Communities).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!createKnowledgeCenter([name], [description], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!createKnowledgeCenter_17r4
documentation:
A knowledge center is a second-level container in Appian Document Management (below Communities).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!createKnowledgeCenter_17r4([name], [description], [securityLevel], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deactivateUser
documentation:
The Deactivate User smart service allows you to select an existing user and disable them from the system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deactivateUser([user], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteDocument
documentation:
The Delete Document node allows you to select a document and then completely remove it from a Knowledge Center or Folder, as well as the server file system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteDocument([document], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteFolder
documentation:
The Delete Folder node allows you to select a folder and then completely remove it from the system, along with its sub-folders.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteFolder([folder], [deleteSubfolders], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteGroup
documentation:
The Delete Group smart service allows you to select a group and remove it from the system.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteGroup([group], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteKnowledgeCenter
documentation:
The Delete KC node allows you to select a Knowledge Center and then completely remove it from the system, along with any child folders.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteKnowledgeCenter([knowledgeCenter], [deleteSubfolders], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteRecords
documentation:
The Delete Records smart service allows you to delete data in your database tables using a record type. If your record type has optimized data access, the changes are automatically synced and removed from the record type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteRecords([records], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteRecords_23r3
documentation:
The Delete Records (23.3) smart service is a powerful tool that allows you to easily delete data from the source system and then automatically sync the changes in Appian.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteRecords_23r3([records], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!deleteFromDataStoreEntities
documentation:
The Delete from Data Store Entities Smart Service lets you delete data in multiple data store entities within the same data store based on your input.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!deleteFromDataStoreEntities([dataToDelete], [deletionComment], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!editDocumentProperties
documentation:
The Edit Document Properties node allows you to update the name and description of a selected document.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!editDocumentProperties([document], [name], [description], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!editGroup
documentation:
The Edit Group smart service allows you to select a group and update any of its available fields, including its name, description, and parent group.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!editGroup([group], [name], [description], [delegatedCreation], [parent], [membershipPolicy], [securityType], [groupPrivacy], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!editKnowledgeCenterProperties
documentation:
The Edit KC node lets you update the name, description and/or expiration date of a selected Knowledge Center.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!editKnowledgeCenterProperties([knowledgeCenter], [name], [description], [expirationDays], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!executeStoredProcedureOnSave
documentation:
The Execute Stored Procedure smart service lets you execute a stored procedure that is defined on any of the Appian supported relational databases that you have connected to.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!executeStoredProcedureOnSave([dataSource], [procedureName], [inputs], [timeout], [autoCommit], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!exportProcessReportToCsv
documentation:
The Export Process Reports to CSV Smart Service allows designers to safely export large datasets.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!exportProcessReportToCsv([report], [filters], [context], [documentName], [documentDescription], [saveInFolder], [documentToUpdate], [includeHeader], [csvDelimiter], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!exportProcessReportToExcel
documentation:
The Export Process Report to Excel Smart Service allows designers to safely export large amounts of data from process reports.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!exportProcessReportToExcel([report], [filters], [context], [documentName], [documentDescription], [saveInFolder], [documentToUpdate], [includeHeader], [sheetName], [sheetNumber], [startingCell], [customCellPositions], [customCellValues], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!exportDataStoreEntityToCsv
documentation:
The Export Data Store Entity to CSV Smart Service allows designers to safely export large datasets.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!exportDataStoreEntityToCsv([entity], [selection], [aggregation], [filters], [documentName], [documentDescription], [saveInFolder], [documentToUpdate], [includeHeader], [csvDelimiter], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!exportDataStoreEntityToExcel
documentation:
The Export Data Store Entity to Excel Smart Service allows designers to safely export large datasets.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!exportDataStoreEntityToExcel([entity], [selection], [aggregation], [filters], [documentName], [documentDescription], [saveInFolder], [documentToUpdate], [includeHeader], [sheetName], [sheetNumber], [startingCell], [customCellPositions], [customCellValues], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!lockDocument
documentation:
The Lock Document node allows you to select and check out a document so that no one else can upload a new version until it has been unlocked.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!lockDocument([document], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!modifyFolderSecurity
documentation:
The Modify Folder Security smart service allows you to select a user to be added to a specified folder as a reader, author, and/or administrator.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!modifyFolderSecurity([folder], [readers], [authors], [administrators], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!modifyKnowledgeCenterSecurity
documentation:
The Modify KC Security node allows you to select users and groups to be added to a Knowledge Center with one of a specific set of security roles.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!modifyKnowledgeCenterSecurity([knowledgeCenter], [readers], [authors], [administrators], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!modifyUserSecurity
documentation:
This smart service allows you to overwrite a specific user's entire role map with different users and groups.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!modifyUserSecurity([group], [administrators], [editors], [viewers], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!moveDocument
documentation:
The Move Document smart service allows you to select a document and move it to a different document folder or Knowledge Center.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!moveDocument([document], [destination], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!moveFolder
documentation:
The Move Folder node allows you to select a folder and move it to a new Knowledge Center or folder (which must already exist).

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!moveFolder([folder], [destinationFolder], [destinationKC], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!reactivateUser
documentation:
The Reactivate User smart service allows you to select a user whose access to the system was previously disabled and re-enable the user account.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!reactivateUser([user], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!removeGroupAdmins
documentation:
The Remove Group Admins node allows you to select one or more users within a group who have administrative rights over the group, and remove those rights.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!removeGroupAdmins([group], [administratorsToRemove], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!removeGroupMembers
documentation:
The Remove Group Members node allows you to select a user within a group, and remove that user from the group.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!removeGroupMembers([group], [members], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!editFolderProperties
documentation:
The Rename Folder node allows you to select a folder and rename it.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!editFolderProperties([folder], [name], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!renameUsers
documentation:
The Rename Users Node allows you to rename existing users on the system while executing your process application.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!renameUsers([oldUsernames], [newUsernames], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!sendPushNotification
documentation:
The Send Push Notification smart service is used to send notifications to one or more Appian for Mobile Devices application users

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!sendPushNotification([recipients], [title], [body], [link], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!setGroupAttributes
documentation:
The Set Group Attributes smart service allows you to modify attribute values for the selected group.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!setGroupAttributes([group], [attributes], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!startProcess
documentation:
The Start Process Smart Service allows you to initiate a process from another process, a web API, or an interface.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!startProcess([processModel], [processParameters], [isSynchronous], [onSuccess], [onError], [onIncomplete])
```

label: a!startProcess_24r3
documentation:
The Start Process Smart Service (24.3) allows you to initiate a process from another process, a web API, or an interface.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!startProcess_24r3([processModel], [processParameters], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!startRuleTestsAll
documentation:
Executes test cases configured for all expression rules within an Appian system from web APIs and Interfaces

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!startRuleTestsAll([onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!startRuleTestsApplications
documentation:
Executes the test cases configured for all expression rules in the specified Appian application(s) from web APIs and interfaces.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!startRuleTestsApplications([applications], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!syncRecords
documentation:
The Sync Records smart service allows you to sync a record or set of records in the specified record type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!syncRecords([recordType], [identifiers], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!syncRecords_22r2
documentation:
The Sync Records smart service allows you to sync a record or set of records in the specified record type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!syncRecords_22r2([recordType], [identifiers], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!unlockDocument
documentation:
The Unlock Document node allows you to select a document that has previously been locked and alter the settings so that it becomes accessible.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!unlockDocument([document], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!updateUserProfile
documentation:
The Update User Profile Service Node allows you to update a selected user's personal data, including their name, address, phone number, supervisor and title.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!updateUserProfile([group], [overwriteAllFields], [firstName], [middleName], [lastName], [nickname], [supervisor], [title], [email], [officePhone], [mobilePhone], [homePhone], [address1], [address2], [address3], [city], [state], [province], [zipCode], [country], [locale], [timeZone], [calendar], [customField1], [customField2], [customField3], [customField4], [customField5], [customField6], [customField7], [customField8], [customField9], [customField10], [onSuccess], [onError*])
```

label: a!writeRecords
documentation:
The Write Records smart service allows you to insert or update data in your database tables using a record type. If your record type has optimized data access, any new or updated data is automatically synced in the record type.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!writeRecords([records], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!writeRecords_23r2
documentation:
The Write Records smart service is a powerful tool that allows you to easily insert or update data in your source system and then automatically sync those changes in Appian.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!writeRecords_23r2([records], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!writeRecords_23r4
documentation:
The Write Records smart service is a powerful tool that allows you to easily insert or update data in your source system and then automatically sync those changes in Appian.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!writeRecords_23r4([records], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!writeToDataStoreEntity
documentation:
The Write to Data Store Entity Smart Service adds process data to an entity in a Data Store.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!writeToDataStoreEntity([dataStoreEntity], [valueToStore], [onSuccess], [onError])
```

label: a!writeToMultipleDataStoreEntities
documentation:
The Write to Multiple Data Store Entities Smart Service writes multiple CDT values to multiple entities within the same data store based on your input.

**Syntax:**
```appian
a!writeToMultipleDataStoreEntities([valuesToStore], [onSuccess], [onError])
```
